summary: This change removes the ownership authorization check and replaces a soft delete with a permanent hard delete. Both are serious: any authenticated user can now permanently destroy another user's record.

findings.title: Authorization check removed before delete
findings.detail: The `record.owner_id != request.user.id` guard was deleted, so any authenticated user can delete any record by id (IDOR).
findings.suggestion: Restore the ownership check (or an equivalent permission check) before deleting.

findings.title: Hard delete replaces recoverable soft delete
findings.detail: Switching from soft_delete() to delete() makes the operation irreversible; previously recoverable records are now permanently destroyed.
findings.suggestion: Keep soft_delete() unless there is an explicit, reviewed retention decision.
